ops.title: When redesigning shared machinery, propose the single from-scratch correct way + migrate everyone — never additive candidates, fallback orders, or dual grammars
ops.body:
Rejecting a dedup plan that APPENDED a 5th entry to capt-hook's MANIFEST_CANDIDATES ("append LAST so legacy repos keep resolving"), the user said: *"we dont want 2 ways, we want exactly 1 correct way, with no backwards compat, then migrate everyhrone. how hard is this to understand"* — after having already said *"pick the right thing that is as if we had designed it from the start, and then just manually migrate everyone"*.

**Why:** An additive candidate/fallback is itself a second way — the exact smell being removed. AGENTS.md already bans backwards-compat layers in code ("No defensive coding"); the user applies the same rule to *design proposals*: probing orders, dual grammars, dual-channel duplication all count. Breakage windows during migration are explicitly acceptable; the fleet is theirs to migrate manually.

**How to apply:** In plans for shared machinery (capt-hook, ccx, cc-guides, fleet conventions): pick the one canonical shape, DELETE the old resolution paths/grammars in the same change (major-version it), and include a migrate-everyone phase — never "keep the old path working during transition" unless the user asks for it. Related: [[guides-fleet-remigration]], [[cc-guides-fragment-pipeline]].
